key_themes: Strong 2026 guidance (9-10% revenue growth, ~14% EPS growth)
YTD stock pullback (down significantly early 2026) creating perceived value/buy-the-dip opportunity
Premium card momentum, high ROE, membership fees, and affluent consumer strength
Upcoming Q1 2026 earnings (April 23) as key catalyst with solid expectations
Balanced by concerns over consumer spending, competition, AI impact, and potential card surcharges
summary: American Express reported strong 2025 results and issued upbeat 2026 guidance for mid-to-high single-digit revenue growth and approximately 14% EPS growth at the midpoint, driven by premium products, membership fees, and resilient affluent spending. The stock experienced a material YTD decline in early 2026 (at one point 12-18% amid AI and consumer fears), but has recovered somewhat to around $332 recently, with consensus analyst targets generally in the mid-$350s and a Hold rating overall.[[1]](https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AXP/history/)[[2]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/american-express-stock-fallen-12-193100754.html)[[3]](https://tickernerd.com/stock/axp-forecast/) Investor and social sentiment is mildly positive, viewing the pullback as a value entry ahead of Q1 earnings on April 23, though discussions note risks around premium consumer health and competitive/regulatory pressures.[[4]](https://www.reddit.com/r/ValueInvesting/comments/1sdlqip/axp_appears_mispriced_at_current_levels/)[[5]](https://www.reddit.com/r/ValueInvesting/comments/1rvs634/axp_amexians_sound_off_please/)
sources: 15+ web news/articles (Yahoo Finance, Motley Fool, Seeking Alpha, MarketBeat), 15+ recent X posts (earnings buzz, bullish targets, comparisons to V/MA), Multiple Reddit threads (r/ValueInvesting, r/amex) discussing mispricing and risks
